commit.message:
fix: use generated image when thumbnail is a data URI

Crawlers (iMessage Applebot, Discordbot, etc.) cannot fetch data: URIs —
they make HTTP requests to external URLs. When Aws:BucketName is not
configured the storage service falls back to a base64 data URI, which
was placed verbatim in og:image and silently ignored by every crawler.

Changes:
- OgController: treat ThumbnailUrl values that don't start with 'http'
  as missing; fall back to the /og/events/{id}/image generated gradient.
- nginx.conf: add comment clarifying that iMessage uses Applebot UA so
  the existing UA map covers it without any extra entries.
- e2e/app.spec.ts: update thumbnail routing test to branch on whether
  the stored URL is a data URI or a real S3 URL, matching the new logic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[minor][feature]: Add more execution flow tag point (#1694)

## PR Title Format

**Required Format:** `[Keyword1] [Keyword2]: Description`

- **Keyword1:** `major`, `minor`, or `patch` (case-insensitive)
- **Keyword2:** `feature`, `bugfix`, `engg`, or `tests`
(case-insensitive)

**Examples:**
- `[MAJOR] [Feature]: new API`
- `[minor] [bugfix]: fix crash`
- `[PATCH][tests]:add coverage`

## Proposed changes


This pull request introduces comprehensive execution flow logging across
multiple controller and request classes in the `IdentityCore` module.
The changes add logging tags at key decision points and completion
blocks, with contextual information such as error codes and correlation
IDs. This enhancement will improve traceability and debugging for silent
and interactive token requests, as well as SSO extension requests.

### Execution flow logging enhancements

* Added `MSIDExecutionFlowLogger` and `MSIDExecutionFlowConstants`
imports to `MSIDRequestControllerFactory.m`,
`MSIDSSORemoteInteractiveTokenRequest.m`, and
`MSIDSSORemoteSilentTokenRequest.m` to enable logging functionality.

fix: reliable image clipboard capture (screenshots, browsers, Snipping Tool)

4 root causes fixed:

1. _clipboard_has_exclude_flag() was calling OpenClipboard() before Qt read the
   clipboard — this broke lazy-render sources (Snipping Tool, Win+Shift+S, some
   apps that render on-demand). IsClipboardFormatAvailable() does NOT require
   OpenClipboard; removed the unnecessary open/close entirely.

2. mime.hasImage() missed CF_BITMAP / CF_DIB from some sources (Snipping Tool,
   Paint, certain games). Added explicit Win32 IsClipboardFormatAvailable() check
   for CF_DIB(8), CF_DIBV5(17), CF_BITMAP(2) as authoritative fallback.

3. clipboard.image() (Qt's QImage conversion) fails for some Win32 colour formats
   (palettized, premultiplied alpha, etc.). Added PIL ImageGrab.grabclipboard()
   as a second attempt — reads CF_DIB directly and converts via Pillow.

4. img.sizeInBytes() returns 0 in PyQt6 for certain QImage formats, causing the
   hash buffer to be empty and the dedup hash to silently not update. Changed to
   img.bytesPerLine() * img.height() which is always correct.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

strongly-typed values. .NET uses a readonly struct following the

via IntelliSense/autocomplete.

Skip zero-padded rows in Mercury for odd log_n (#467)

When log_n is odd, Mercury pads f_poly from 2^log_n to 2^(log_n+1) with zeros,
creating a b×b matrix where the upper half of rows are all zeros. This wastes
~50% of field arithmetic in divide_by_binomial and quot_f.

Compute b_row = original_size / b (the actual nonzero row count) and use it to:
- compute_h_poly: process only b_row rows, then pad result to b
- divide_by_binomial: operate on f_poly[..original_size] with b_row rows
- quot_f: build from f_poly[..original_size] instead of full padded poly

The division identity f(X) = (X^b - alpha) * q(X) + g(X) still holds because
the upper rows of f are zero. The verifier is completely unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

operations.

fix: macOS notifications now open console when clicked (#1649)

* Initial plan

* 🐛 fix: call event.preventDefault() in notification onclick to fix macOS opening Finder

Without event.preventDefault(), macOS handles the notification click
with its default OS behavior, which can launch Finder or the app icon
location instead of focusing the browser window and navigating to the
console deep link URL.

Fixed in both sendNotificationWithDeepLink (useDeepLink.ts) and the
inline notification handler in MiniDashboard.tsx.

commit.message:
fix(packaging): include hwdb and quirks in deb/rpm/tarball packages (#82)

Linux deb, rpm, and tarball packages were missing the hwdb file
(joystick classification) and modprobe quirks file (ALWAYS_POLL for
Asetek/Simagic). Without these, installed packages would not
provide full plug-and-play support.

Changes:
- Tarball: include hwdb and quirks files alongside udev rules
- Deb: install to /etc/udev/hwdb.d/ and /etc/modprobe.d/
- RPM: install to same paths with config(noreplace) protection
- Both deb and rpm postinst scripts now run systemd-hwdb update
- Tarball README updated with complete manual install steps

commit.message:
feat(packaging): add Linux hwdb entries for joystick classification (#79)

Add 99-racing-wheel-suite.hwdb with ID_INPUT_JOYSTICK=1 entries for all
supported racing peripherals. Without these entries, Linux may
misclassify devices (especially pedals) as accelerometers instead of
joysticks, breaking SDL/Proton/Steam game detection.

Covers 90+ devices across 20+ manufacturers:
- All wheelbases (FFB devices)
- All pedals, shifters, handbrakes, and button boxes

Also updates install.sh to install the hwdb file and run
systemd-hwdb update, and adds packaging validation tests.

Source: JacKeTUs/simracing-hwdb format specification

* Update styles.css

* Fix Android shell: channel completed prematurely by stderr reader

Both stdout and stderr readers independently called TryComplete() on the
shared channel. If stderr closed early (no error output), it completed
the channel, preventing any further stdout data from being delivered.

Fix: track active reader count and only complete the channel when ALL
readers are done using Interlocked.Decrement.

* Cache brush values to work around MAUI ToString() losing gradient stops

commit.message:
[#10168] Fail fast when file: URI source is missing in JobManager.fetchFileFromUri (#10212)

### What changes were proposed in this pull request?

Validate that the source path exists before creating a symlink in
`fetchFileFromUri`. Previously, a dangling symlink was silently created
when the source file did not exist, causing failures downstream instead
of at fetch time.

### Why are the changes needed?

`JobManager.fetchFileFromUri` currently reports success for `file:` URIs
by creating a symlink in the staging directory, even when the source
path does not exist. This creates a dangling symlink and returns a
seemingly valid staged path, causing failures later when the file is
consumed instead of failing at fetch time.

### Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?

No. This is an internal defensive check — the external API is unchanged.
Jobs that previously failed late with a confusing error will now fail
early with a clear message.

### How was this patch tested?

Added unit test `testFetchFileFromUriWithMissingLocalFileShouldFail` (as
suggested in #10168) that:
1. Creates a staging directory
2. Constructs a `file:` URI pointing to a non-existent path
3. Asserts that `fetchFileFromUri` throws `RuntimeException`

Closes #10168

---------

fix: cache generated OG images in-process with IMemoryCache

ImageSharp PNG encoding was running on every /og/events/{id}/image request
with no server-side cache. On a cold EKS pod (t3.small) this exceeded the
30s test timeout. The HTTP Cache-Control headers were set but no
UseResponseCaching middleware was registered, so ASP.NET was regenerating
the image on every hit.

Fix: inject IMemoryCache into OgController and cache rendered PNG bytes
keyed by event ID (TTL 24h, SizeLimit 500 entries). The gradient is
deterministic from the event ID so caching is always correct.

Also revert the band-aid test.setTimeout(60s) added in the previous commit.
